title: Hubertia andringitrensis
taxonomy:
Hubertia andringitrensis is classified within the family Asteraceae. 
This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Hubertia. 
Hubertia andringitrensis is native to the island of Madagascar. 
The species is part of the tribe Cardueae, which is a subtribe of the family Asteraceae. 
Hubertia andringitrensis is an important part of the island's native flora.

intro: Hubertia andringitrensis, a species of plant in the family Asteraceae, is a medicinal herb native to Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Hubertia andringitrensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
